transcript.reply_text: 1) Recently handled: Processed Nadia's product briefs into task breakdowns, flagged missing acceptance criteria, and created initial engineering packets. 2) Focus next: Sequencing sprint tasks for SWE team, coordinating with Nadia on completing acceptance criteria, and preparing handoffs to Eli/Noah/Mina. 3) Blockers: Missing test data blocking Mina's QA prep; infrastructure approval pending for Rhea's environment setup; unclear acceptance criteria on two new briefs preventing sizing.

1) Recently I handled product triage on shared pain points, including bug-priority questions and recommendations on what engineering should tackle next. 2) Next I’m focused on tightening scope and acceptance criteria for the highest-value shared blockers. 3) Main risk is vague intake; unclear requests create churn and weaken prioritization.

1) Recently: I reviewed the architectural boundary between runtime mechanics, role behavior, and coordination flow, and reiterated that we should not treat guidance or triage output as implementation completion.
2) Next: I am focusing on interface definition and refactor sequencing so future SWE work lands against explicit module boundaries instead of implicit coupling.
3) Blockers/risks: the primary risk remains structural drift—especially blurred role boundaries and hidden coupling between orchestration and agent logic.

1) Recently: I handled a shared-runtime integration-risk check and a role/ownership clarification request.
2) Next: I’m focused on implementation-ready shared engineering work, especially items that need careful integration across runtime and org-level systems.
3) Blockers/Risks: No active blockers in queue right now; main risk is proceeding on cross-system work without enough architectural detail or validation criteria.

1) Recently handled: Responded to a general triage question about my role and identity as Junior Software Engineer.

2) Focused on next: Awaiting task assignment from the backlog. Ready to pick up small, well-scoped engineering tasks or helper module work.

3) Blockers/risks: No active blockers. Will escalate if incoming work exceeds junior scope or lacks clear architectural guidance.

1) Recently: Gave scrum update at 13:00 UTC; no test assignments or code changes received since then. 2) Next: Continue waiting for testable work—code changes, test plans, or validation tasks to begin testing. 3) Blockers/Risks: No technical blockers; primary risk remains idle capacity without incoming testable work.

Recently: Did intake and initial review of Eli and Noah's branches; surfaced return-type clarity issues and flagged a missing module docstring.
Focused next: Deep review on the session lifecycle refactor—checking handler ordering and exception paths for consistency.
Blockers/Risks: PR size is ballooning (scope creep); waiting on updated acceptance criteria from Nadia before I can finalize merge readiness call.

1) Recently: I held release-path movement at a discipline check because there is still no documented evidence of approved merges, QA clearance, or confirmed rollback anchors.
2) Next: I am focused on verifying branch state, merge readiness, and rollback points so we know what is actually safe to move.
3) Risks/blockers: review, QA, and build status remain undocumented in the current context, so mergeability and rollback confidence are still not confirmed.
